translation: Q3 has hataf patah. Other MSS and MAM agree: shewa, no ga'aya. Prints add ga'aya.
LC missing hataf dots in het (patah instead of hataf patah). Typist corrected silently.
Question whether AC(S) has hataf patah in shin. Answer: no, shewa only, no meteg in vav.
Question whether AC(S) has meteg, hataf patah, and segol. Answer: no meteg, shewa only in het, tsere in mem.
Typist noted unusual form. Possibly hataf patah as in Venice MG (corrected in MS). Noted in BHS.
LC has shewa instead of hataf in ayin.
AC(S) and LC confirm: no hataf in resh.
LC missing hataf dots in ayin. Prints have maqaf-joined word with ga'aya.
LC surprisingly has hataf dots in het and right ga'aya (MAM has plain patah).
LC surprisingly has right ga'aya and hataf dots in het (MAM has plain patah).
AC(S) etc: hataf patah with ga'aya. LC missing hataf dots in interrogative he. Prints: hataf patah, no ga'aya.
LC has shewa in het instead of hataf patah.
Discussion of ga'aya placement before segol + hataf segol. Various MS readings differ on ga'aya position.
LC has hataf patah in he (MAM has patah). Typist did not note this.
LC has hataf patah in ayin (MAM has patah).
Question: hataf patah in shin? Answer: no, shewa only. No hataf.
LC missing hataf dots in het.
LC has hataf qamats marking qamats qatan. Hataf dots clearly visible in color photo. Breuer concurs. Dotan and typist read plain qamats.
LC has hataf patah in het (MAM has plain patah).
LC has hataf qamats, no dagesh in shin. Venice MG also lacks dagesh. MAM follows MS et al.
LC missing hataf dots in alef (MAM has hataf segol).
Dotan uncertain about LC reading in alef — possibly hataf, with an erasure mark.
LC has hataf patah in ayin (MAM has plain patah).
LC has hataf patah in vav (MAM has shewa).
LC missing hataf dots in alef (MAM has hataf patah).
LC may have hataf qamats instead of hataf patah in alef. Hard to discern in photo; possibly a stain.
LC and L3 missing hataf qamats dots in dalet.
LC may have hataf hiriq per typist. Dotan and Breuer printed hiriq+ga'aya without comment. Mark may be ga'aya.
LC missing hataf dots in alef (MAM has hataf patah).
LC ketiv missing hataf dots in ayin.
LC missing hataf dots in het.

target_word: הַהַהגה/ג לְיְהֹוָה֙בספרי תימן הַֽלְיהֹוָה֙הַֽלְיהֹוָה֙ה.....גה/ג בתיבה אחתאאא*
translation: Complex multi-source discussion of הַלְיהֹוָה. LC has hataf patah in he as separate word. L1 has hataf patah in yod. Various readings.
AC has hataf patah in both doubled letters (bet, nun). MAM and other MSS have no hataf, no ga'aya.
AC surprisingly has hataf patah in alef.
AC surprisingly missing hataf dots in ayin. MAM, LC, and prints have hataf patah.
AC surprisingly missing hataf dots in alef.
AC and LC have shewa. Some prints/Koren: hataf qamats. Q: hataf patah. MS debated all three options. MG: left shin dot.
AC has hataf qamats marking qamats qatan. See Radak and Minchat Shai.
LC surprisingly has hataf patah in ayin (MAM has plain patah).
Per Breuer, AC has hataf in non-guttural letter (bet). But may be a stain, or hataf patah with patah line erased.
AC has hataf qamats marking qamats qatan. Also in first-edition MG and MGH.
Per Breuer, AC has hataf qamats for qamats qatan. Qamats visible; possible faded hataf dots.
AC has hataf qamats marking qamats qatan. Also in MGH.
AC surprisingly has segol instead of hataf segol in alef. MGH concurs.
LC missing hataf qamats dots in alef (has plain qamats instead).
Discussion of whether qamats is qatan. Radak says it should reduce. Ga'aya present. Opinions differ (Weinberg, Davidson vs. Koren, Simanim).
LC ketiv surprisingly has hataf patah dots in ayin.
LC missing hataf patah dots in second he.
AC has hataf hiriq in shin.
AC has hataf hiriq in tav.
Discussion of qamats qatan. MS cites hataf for the dagesh. Da'at Miqra: qamats qatan replacing qubuts in pu'al.
Discussion of whether qamats is gadol or qatan. Sephardi grammarians cite tradition of qamats qatan (hataf). MS concurs despite guttural ayin.
LC has segol in shin + hataf patah in yod (MAM: hataf segol in shin + shewa in yod). Q-M: segol + ga'aya.
LC has hataf in qof.
AC etc: hataf patah + ga'aya. LC has plain patah in he. Breuer notes LC uses patah for hataf in ga'aya contexts.
LC surprisingly has hataf segol in shin (MAM has shewa).
LC has hataf patah in het (MAM has plain patah).
System-A reading has hataf patah. LC has ga'aya. Discussion of marka vs. ga'aya across three parallel passages.
AC has hataf patah (as MAM). LC missing hataf dots in ayin.
LC may lack hataf patah in het (shewa instead). But patah line likely exists; collides with lamed in line below.
LC missing hataf dots in alef (MAM has hataf segol).
LC has phonetic ga'aya + rafe on pe (mobile shewa). Petersburg MS has hataf in tsade. MS notes some MSS have hataf in tsade.
LC etc: hataf qamats in dalet. Prints: shewa. Applies throughout Esther.
LC has hataf patah dots in ayin (MAM has plain patah).
LC may have hataf qamats in alef (MAM has hataf patah). But dot is different color — may be a stain.
Q-M etc: hataf segol (as MAM). LC has hataf patah in alef instead.
Hataf segol in gimel marks morphological segol vowel. Cf. verse 19.
LC has hataf patah in ayin (MAM has plain patah).
LC may have hataf patah in yod. Breuer marked with doubt — smudged, odd line under yod.
LC has hataf qamats dots in he.
LC has hataf qamats in qof to mark the morphological holam vowel preserved in spelling. Cf. next verse.
LC has hataf dots in ayin (MAM has plain patah).
LC may have hataf patah in resh. Breuer: yes. Dotan: uncertain. Examination shows no clear hataf line.
AC surprisingly has shewa in vav + patah in he (MAM: hataf patah in he). Possibly hataf dots belong to he with vav unpointed.
AC and S1 have hataf qamats marking qamats qatan. LC has plain qamats (as prints).
